feed.title: Frontier at Flat Rate
feed.subtitle: Own your AI agent. Stop renting it. Proof points, analysis, and the economics of self-hosted vs. subscription AI.

entry.title: Your Copilot Budget Is a 3-Month Illusion — The Enterprise Squeeze | Frontier at Flat Rate
entry.summary: GitHub's promotional credits expire September 2026. Business users lose 61% of credits. Enterprise loses 64%. No annual lock-in available. Your real costs start in 3 months.

entry.title: AI Billing Was Designed to Obfuscate — And June 15 Proves It
entry.summary: PCMag reports AI subscription pricing was 'heavily subsidized to encourage adoption and obfuscate the financial costs.' The subsidy era ends June 15. Here's what it means for developers.

entry.title: The Productivity Tax — When Your Best Day Costs the Most | Frontier at Flat Rate
entry.summary: Metered AI billing means your most productive day is your most expensive day. The harder you work, the more you pay. That's not a feature — it's a tax on ambition.

entry.title: Three Billing Changes in Five Months — When Your AI Vendor Can't Stop Reworking Your Bill | Frontier at Flat Rate
entry.summary: Anthropic changed billing four times in five months. GitHub Copilot switched to tokens overnight. When vendors rework your bill this often, who's really in control of your costs?

entry.title: When the Floor Disappears: Why AI Vendors Are Removing Your Safety Nets | The AI You Built
entry.summary: Three AI vendors independently converged on the same pattern: remove the fallback, make safety nets opt-in, then make them disappear entirely. Here's what it means for your AI bill.

entry.title: They Changed the Contract: Annual Copilot Users Hit With Stealth Multiplier Increases
entry.summary: GitHub quietly changed model multipliers on annual Copilot subscribers on June 1. Claude Opus went from 7.5x to 27x. Your contract didn't change — what it buys did.

entry.title: The AI Rent Receipt: What You Actually Spend on AI Subscriptions in 2026
entry.summary: GitHub Copilot goes 10x-50x. Claude Pro splits your credits. An itemized breakdown of what renting your AI actually costs — and what owning it looks like instead.

entry.title: You're Renting Your AI. Here's How You Can Tell.
entry.summary: Anthropic restricted third-party agent access and dropped team-tier pricing overnight. Here's the pattern nobody's naming — and the 4 signs you're renting your AI instead of owning it.

entry.title: Day One: The Rent Already Arrived — GitHub Copilot Token Billing Live
entry.summary: June 1, 2026. GitHub Copilot's token billing went live today. Bills jumped 10x-50x. The safety net is gone. Two vendors, two weeks, the same word: sustainable.

entry.title: Two Vendors, Two Weeks, Two Price Hikes — How to Own Your AI Before June 15
entry.summary: GitHub Copilot goes 9x on June 1. Anthropic splits your Claude credits on June 15. Here's the 3-step playbook to own your AI before the rent goes up again.

entry.title: The Opt-In Trap — Why Anthropic Makes You Claim Credits You Already Paid For
entry.summary: Anthropic's June 15 credit split requires developers to actively claim credits they already pay for. If you don't opt in, your programmatic AI access stops. Here's the full picture.

entry.title: The Safety Net They Removed — Why GitHub Copilot Removed Your Only Protection
entry.summary: GitHub Copilot didn't just raise prices on June 1. They removed the fallback model, killed annual plans, and started billing you on two meters at once. Here's what that means.

entry.title: The $2 Student Who Proved AI Billing Is Broken — GitHub Copilot Token Billing
entry.summary: A CS student burned through 200 Copilot credits in one morning — 10 to 20 requests. Whether you pay $2 or $100,000, rent is rent. 12 days until Anthropic changes your AI bill on June 15.

entry.title: You're Not Tired of AI. You're Tired of Renting It. | Frontier at Flat Rate
entry.summary: AI fatigue is real — 1,700+ people upvoted 'I'm Tired of Talking to AI' on Hacker News. But the fatigue isn't with AI itself. It's with renting your intelligence from platforms that can change the terms, raise the price, or cut you off tomorrow.

entry.title: The Landlord Is Selling the Building — Anthropic's IPO Proves AI Rent Is Structural
entry.summary: Anthropic filed for a $965 billion IPO on the same day GitHub switched to token billing. Revenue went from $9B to $47B in six months. Your rent is the IPO fuel.

entry.title: June 15 Is Coming — Why Your AI Agent Might Stop Working
entry.summary: On June 15, Anthropic splits Claude subscription credits. OpenClaw, Cursor, and other third-party agents get a separate pool. Here's what breaks.

entry.title: Claude Opus 4.8 and the Effort Tax: Why Your AI Vendor Controls How Hard Your AI Thinks
entry.summary: Claude Opus 4.8 adds effort control — your vendor now decides how much thinking your AI does. Fast mode costs 3x less, proving the base price is inflated. Here's why this is the clearest Rent signal yet.

entry.title: Same Model, Same Treadmill: What Claude Opus 4.8's "Effort Control" Reveals About AI Pricing
entry.summary: Claude Opus 4.8 adds effort control — pick how hard the model thinks. It's the clearest admission yet that token billing is fundamentally broken.

entry.title: The $1 Trillion Invoice: Who's Paying for the AI Build-Out?
entry.summary: Big Tech spent $1 trillion on AI infrastructure. Now the bill is arriving — at your company, in your inbox, on your credit card. Josh Bersin explains who pays.

entry.title: The $200 Door That Won't Open — Copilot Max and the 6-Step Subscription Trap
entry.summary: GitHub launched Copilot Max at $200/month — but new users still can't sign up. 43 days of closed doors. The complete 6-step subscription trap, documented in real time.

entry.title: The 24x Hidden Price Range — Copilot Auto Mode Charges 24x More Without Telling You
entry.summary: Copilot Auto Mode picks the model — and the price. Same task can cost 24x more depending on which model is selected. You have no visibility. June 15: Anthropic does the same thing to your credits.

entry.title: The Failure Tax — Why You Pay Twice for AI in 2026
entry.summary: AI doesn't just fail — you pay for the failure AND the fix. Token billing means vendors profit from errors. Here's why the Failure Tax is the hidden cost nobody talks about.

entry.title: Copilot Alternatives in 2026: Flat-Rate AI Tools That Won't Surprise-Bill You
entry.summary: GitHub Copilot token billing starts June 1, 2026. Compare 7 flat-rate AI coding alternatives — no tokens, no metering, no bill shock. Full pricing breakdown.

entry.title: Copilot Billing Changes June 2026: What Developers Need to Know
entry.summary: GitHub Copilot switches to token billing June 1, 2026. Here's what changes, how much more you'll pay, and the best alternatives with flat-rate pricing.

entry.title: Flat-Rate AI vs Token Billing: The Developer's Guide to 2026 Pricing
entry.summary: Token billing, per-seat licensing, usage tiers — AI pricing is fracturing into two models. Here's what each one actually costs, which vendors use which, and why flat-rate AI is the missing middle developers have been waiting for.

entry.title: When Does Local AI Beat Cloud AI? The 2026 Break-Even Analysis
entry.summary: Outsourcing plus local AI is now more economical than frontier lab APIs for most developer workflows. Here's the math, the break-even points, and when owning your AI infrastructure beats renting it.

entry.title: Copilot Token Billing Starts June 1: What Changes, What It Costs, and Why It Matters
entry.summary: GitHub Copilot adds per-operation token billing on June 1, 2026. Here's what your bill will actually look like — and why the pattern is bigger than one tool.

entry.title: What Happens Monday June 2: Your First Copilot Token Bill
entry.summary: June 2 is the first business day under Copilot token billing. Here's what developers will see, what it means, and why this pattern matters for every AI tool you use.

entry.title: Your AI Just Got 10GB Smaller
entry.summary: Google quietly cut Gmail free storage from 15GB to 5GB. No announcement. No email. Just a support page edit. Here's what it means for your AI agent.

entry.title: Google I/O 2026 Is Over: The Definitive Own vs Rent Scorecard
entry.summary: Post-I/O 2026 synthesis: every announcement, every hidden cost, and why owning your AI agent is the only rational move for solopreneurs after Google's agent blitz.

entry.title: Google I/O 2026 Live: What Just Happened and What It Means for Your AI Agent
entry.summary: Google I/O 2026's biggest announcements prove Big Tech wants to rent you AI agents — not let you own them. Same-day analysis of what they didn't say.

entry.title: Google I/O 2026 Live: What Just Happened and What It Means for Your AI Agent
entry.summary: Same-day analysis of Google I/O 2026 keynote announcements — what Google didn't tell you about owning vs renting your AI agent.

entry.title: Google I/O 2026: Why Your AI Agent Will Never Be Yours
entry.summary: Google's keynote is May 19. The centerpiece isn't search or Android — it's personal AI agents. Here's what we already know before Sundar takes the stage.
